---
title: "Clairvoyant Project, Fall 2025"
author: 
- "Team Joey"
format:
  pdf:
    colorlinks: true
---

## 

```{r Imports}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
final_data_merged <- read_csv("Data/Clean/final_data_merged.csv")
```

```{r Graph1}
ggplot(final_data_merged, 
       aes(x = Industry, y = Industry_Health_Score)) +
  stat_summary(fun = "mean", geom = "bar", fill = "blue") +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Health Score",
    x = "Industry",
    y = "Health Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),                 # removes all grid lines
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

```

```{r Graph2}
ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Industry_Vol_12m, 
                              y = Industry_Return_12m,
                              color = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    x = "Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "12-Month Return (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

```

**Interpretation:**\

Top-left = strong returns with low risk → healthiest industries.\

Bottom-right = poor returns, high risk → weak industries.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

final_data_merged %>%
  filter(Industry %in% c("Communication Services", "Aerospace & Defense")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Company_Vol_12m, 
             y = Company_Return_12m, 
             color = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Company), vjust = -1, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Company Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    subtitle = "Focused on Communication Services and Aerospace & Defense",
    x = "Company Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "12-Month Return (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )


```

```{r}
library(GGally)
df <- final_data_merged %>%
  select(Industry_Return_3m, Industry_Return_12m, Industry_Vol_12m, Industry_Health_Score)
ggcorr(df, label = TRUE)

```

```{r}
ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Company_Vol_12m, 
                              y = Company_Return_12m,
                              color = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    x = "Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "12-Month Return (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Filter to just Communication Services and Aerospace & Defense
plot_data <- final_data_merged %>%
  filter(Industry %in% c("Communication Services", "Aerospace & Defense"))

# Add a highlight flag for Verizon
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(highlight = ifelse(grepl("Verizon", Company, ignore.case = TRUE), "Verizon", "Other"))

# Plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Company_Vol_12m, y = Company_Return_12m)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = highlight, size = highlight), alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Verizon" = "red", "Other" = "gray70")) +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Verizon" = 4, "Other" = 2)) +
  geom_text(
    data = subset(plot_data, highlight == "Verizon"),
    aes(label = Company),
    vjust = -1,
    color = "red",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Company Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    subtitle = "Highlighting Verizon Communications Inc.",
    x = "Company Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "Company Return (1-Year)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  ) +
  # ✅ Extend the x-axis range to add more space on the left
  expand_limits(x = min(plot_data$Company_Vol_12m, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.8)



```

```{r}
ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Company_Vol_12m, 
                              y = Company_Return_12m,
                              color = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    x = "Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "Return (1-Year))"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Industry, y = Industry_Health_Score)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "col", fill = "#1f77b4") +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Health Score",
    x = "Industry",
    y = "Health Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12,base_family = "Helvetica" ) +  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

```
```{r}
ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Industry_Vol_12m, 
                              y = Industry_Return_12m,
                              color = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = , alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    x = "Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "12-Month Return (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 9)
  )


```

```{r}
ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Company_Vol_12m, 
                              y = Company_Return_12m,
                              color = Industry)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "Company Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    x = "Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "Return (1-Year)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 9)
  )

```









```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Filter to just Communication Services and Aerospace & Defense
plot_data <- final_data_merged %>%
  filter(Industry %in% c("Communication Services", "Aerospace & Defense"))

# Add a highlight flag for Verizon
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(highlight = ifelse(grepl("Verizon", Company, ignore.case = TRUE), "Verizon", "Other"))

# Plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Company_Vol_12m, y = Company_Return_12m)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = highlight, size = highlight), alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Verizon" = "red", "Other" = "gray70")) +
  scale_size_manual(values = c("Verizon" = 4, "Other" = 2)) +
  geom_text(
    data = subset(plot_data, highlight == "Verizon"),
    aes(label = Company),
    vjust = -1,
    color = "red",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Company Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    subtitle = "Highlighting Verizon Communications Inc.",
    x = "Company Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "Company Return (1-Year)"
  ) +
  expand_limits(x = min(plot_data$Company_Vol_12m, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.8)+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 9),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

```


```{r}
final_data_merged <- final_data_merged %>%
  group_by(Industry) %>%
  mutate(job_postings = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(final_data_merged, aes(x = Industry_Vol_12m, 
                              y = Industry_Return_12m,
                              color = Industry,
                              size = job_postings)) +   # use job_postings here
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#7AC5CD", "#FF7F50", "#BCEE68", "#EEA9b8", "#836FFF")) +
  labs(
    title = "Industry Risk vs. Return (12-Month)",
    x = "Volatility (1-Year)",
    y = "Return (1-Year)",
    size = "Job Postings (Millions)"
  ) +
  #scale_size_continuous(
  #  labels = label_number(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M"),
   # range = c(2, 10)  # optional: tweak point sizes
  #) 

  scale_size_continuous( labels = label_comma())+
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 9)
  )
```
